high_confidence_fixture_categories.candidate_use: case fixture and flowing corpus fixture
high_confidence_fixture_categories.deterministic_candidate: Require repeated adjacent discourse words or duplicate phrase frames, not any learner error.
Only narrow repeated openers such as first/firstly duplication should be candidates.

high_confidence_fixture_categories.candidate_use: flowing corpus fixture and review checklist source
high_confidence_fixture_categories.deterministic_candidate: No single word rule. Only bounded frames where a vague claim asks for evidence but names no evidence.

high_confidence_fixture_categories.candidate_use: source-only unless exact phrase is independently validated
high_confidence_fixture_categories.deterministic_candidate: Use only stable phrase replacements already covered by maintained style sources.

high_confidence_fixture_categories.candidate_use: flowing corpus fixture and metrics calibration
high_confidence_fixture_categories.deterministic_candidate: Metrics thresholds only; avoid claiming every long sentence is bad.

reject_as_default_slopless_rules.reason: They are grammar correction, not slop, and will create dialect and learner-English false positives.

reject_as_default_slopless_rules.pattern: all simplifications from ASSET or MinWikiSplit
reject_as_default_slopless_rules.reason: Simpler is not always better. Slopless should not flatten technical or literary prose by default.

reject_as_default_slopless_rules.pattern: all conclusion or lead labels from PERSUADE
reject_as_default_slopless_rules.reason: These are discourse roles, not errors.
The feedback itself contains generic AI-style coaching language and should be used as risk material.
